summary:
Only two sentences; focus only on clinically essential facts from the patient scenario and options provided:

The infant presents with classic features of tetralogy of Fallot—including central cyanosis, pulmonary outflow tract obstruction, right ventricular hypertrophy, ventricular septal defect, and overriding aorta—which confirms diagnosis prior to surgery.  
Normal hemoglobin levels do not exclude iron deficiency anemia in children with congenital heart disease due to increased metabolic demand and poor nutrition associated with chronic hypoxia, making comprehensive evaluation critical.
